what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-assets channel, binding an attachment id to a target image URI and noting its type and addition timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
added timestamp uses ISO 8601 with microsecond precision and '+00:00' timezone
standouts: Target URI contains a literal space and parentheses in 'image (13)'
It is a channel-scoped attachment ledger entry with no descriptive title or caption
concerns: Target filename 'image (13)' is generic and looks like an auto-generated placeholder
Space and parentheses in the URI may cause parsing/encoding issues
Missing title, alt text, MIME type, file size, checksum, or uploader fields
notes: A channel attachment ledger entry that maps attachment att-46a84c8c to an image asset under design-assets/icons with a creation timestamp.
questions: Is the target URI resolvable by the storage system?
Why is there no title, caption, or alt text for this image attachment?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a metadata record for an image attachment, which is exactly what its fields describe.
intention_signals: Track when and where an image attachment was added to the design-assets channel so it can be retrieved or referenced later.
novelty: The target URI contains an unescaped space and parentheses in the filename segment ('image (13)').
contribution: A concrete attachment-to-asset binding with a timestamp, preserving the original channel context and target reference for an image asset.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record for att-46a84c8c in the design-assets channel, declaring attachment_type images, target image://design-assets/icons/image (13), and an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to catalog an image attachment added to the design-assets channel and link it to its stored image asset.
